content.full_text:
Farn Polrucel PnacrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
December 21,2004
Prosper Acquah, Assistant Treasurer
Ida E. Yarbrough, CPA
5471 South Hillcrest Drive
Los Angeles, CA 90043
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-04-249
Dear Mr. Acquah:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Assemblymember
Mark Ridley-Thomas regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act').' Please note that the Commission does not provide assistance relating to past
conduct. Qn re oglesby (1975) I FPPC ops. 7l and regulation 18329(c)(4XA).)
Therefore, any conclusions contained herein apply only to prospective actions.
QUESTIONS
May checks made out to the Reelect Ridley-Thomas 20M committee received at a
fundraiser for the Reelect Ridley-Thomas 2006 committee be deposited into the 2006
committee's bank account? If not, may the committee deposit the checks into its 2006
accotrnt if contributors sign a declaration stating their intention was to contribute to the
2006 committee and authorizing the committee to deposit the check into its 2006
account?
CONCLUSIONS
The Reelect Ridley-Thomas 20M committee may not deposit checks made out to
that committee into lhe2006 committee's bank account. There is nothing in the Act or
its corresponding regulations that provides a process permitting contributors subsequently
to authorize deposit of the checks into a different account than that for which the check
was written. The Act does permit a contribution to be returned at any time.
I Government Code sectiors 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
6-iil


File No. A-04-249
Page No. 2
FACTS
A flrndraiser was recently orgarized for the Reelect Ridley-Thomas 2006
committee, one of your clients. The fundraiser was organized after the November 2,
2004, election, however, some of the contributors at the fundraising event inadvertently
wrote checks to the Reelect Ridley-Thomx2}}4 committee.
ANALYSIS
Sections 85200 and 85201 require contributions to be deposited in the applicable
campaign account. You ask whether checks made out toone account can be deposited
into another account. There is nothing in the Act that provides a process permiuing a
committee to deposit checks into an account other than that for which the check was
written. The Act does permit a contribution to be returned2 at any time. (Section 85319.)
However, we note that section 85316 provides that:
'A contribution for an election maybe accepted by a candidate for
elective state office after the date of the election only to the extent that the
contribution does not exceed net debts outstanding from the election, and
the contribution does not otherwise exceed the applicable contribution
limit for that election." (Also see regulation lg53l.6l.)
Therefore, if the Reelect Ridley-Thomas 2004 committee had net debt
outstanding at the time it received the checks, the committee may also retain the checks.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
/r.-"-)>r,t -,--r.*-
By: Kevin S. Moen, PhD A-u)
Political Reform Consultant II
Political Reform Division
KSMjg
I:\Advicelfis\A4-249
The provisions ofregulation 18531 apply. As noted, we cannot advise regarding past conduct.
(In re Oglesby (1975) I EPPC Ops. 71.)
